primary_concern: Sepsis with multi-organ dysfunction and acute kidney injury (AKI) progression
clinical_summary: Patient demonstrates moderate neurological impairment (GCS 13, 60% command response) with stable disorientation. Persistent fever (38.5°C) and elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) suggest ongoing infection. AKI has progressed from Stage 1 (2.1 mg/dL) to Stage 3 (8.9 mg/dL) creatinine. Low platelets (88-95 K/uL) and elevated WBC (14.2 K/uL) support systemic inflammation. Urine output remains critically low at 23 mL/hr despite norepinephrine support.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and AKI Stage 3 meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2).
prioritized_risks.threshold: Sepsis-3: suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine rose 6.5 mg/dL in 6 hours (Stage 3 AKI) exceeding KDIGO criteria for rapid renal failure.
prioritized_risks.threshold: KDIGO AKI: creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or urine output <0.5 mL/kg/hr for ≥6h

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulation criteria for organ dysfunction.
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor-dependent MAP criteria not fully documented.
prioritized_risks.threshold: Septic shock: vasopressor-dependent MAP ≥65 with lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Order urgent infectious disease consult for source control evaluation
Escalate AKI management: consider renal replacement therapy for Stage 3 AKI
Monitor platelet trends and consider transfusion if <50 K/uL
Optimize fluid balance while maintaining MAP ≥65 mmHg
doctor_handoff: Review blood/urine cultures for pathogen identification. Confirm AKI etiology (pre-renal vs intrinsic). Reassess neurological response in 4 hours per ICU protocol.

disease_progression.observation: Lactate peaks at 4.2 mmol/L; platelets decline to 88 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
